meta.name: Discussion then ready (Wave 1, issue #134)
meta.description: Exercises the per-submission intent + ready-quorum gate with two players. Turn 1: SOC opens with a discussion question (no advance), CISO answers ready, SOC closes ready — quorum met, AI fires. Turn 2: CISO posts ready first, SOC chimes in discuss (still no advance), then SOC closes ready. Demonstrates the load-bearing Wave 1 affordance: players can talk to each other before the AI moves on. Engine-mode scenario (no ai_messages captured); covered by tests/scenarios/test_scenario_runner.py::test_discussion_then_ready_scenario_runs which drives every submission through the live pipeline and asserts per-submission intent flows end-to-end.

scenario_prompt: Ransomware via vendor portal — finance laptops impacted at 03:14 Wed. Drill: confirm intent gating works.

submissions.content: Quick check before I commit — are we treating the vendor portal as compromised end-to-end, or just the affected laptops? Want to make sure my containment scope matches the call.
Treat the portal as compromised end-to-end. Isolate the affected laptops, pull IR Lead, start the regulator-notification clock.
Acknowledged — disabling the vendor account, pulling Defender event timeline, scoping for lateral movement. Will report in 5.
Stage Comms draft for legal review. Notify board chair via the secure channel.
One thing — Comms might want to wait until we know whether the vendor account exfiltrated anything. Otherwise the holding statement promises we don't know yet.
Initial scope: 3 laptops in finance, all behind the vendor portal. No lateral movement seen yet. Exfil check: pulling proxy logs now, ETA 10 min.
